framing: VP of Operations at NextEra Energy seeking CEO role at late-stage utility-scale battery storage company

jd.title: Chief Executive Officer
jd.company: Form Energy (utility-scale iron-air battery storage, $1.2B+ raised)
jd.style_note: Enterprise: board-level hire, specific energy sector expertise, scale-up leadership

requirements.text: 20+ years of experience in energy, utilities, or energy storage with progressive leadership
Track record as CEO, President, or COO of energy company with $100M+ revenue
Experience scaling manufacturing operations from pilot to commercial production
Deep relationships with US utility executives, PUCs (Public Utility Commissions), and FERC/DOE stakeholders
Experience with IRA (Inflation Reduction Act) incentives: 45X manufacturing credits, ITC, and PTC structures
Board governance experience (public company or PE/VC-backed board)
Experience with battery storage, long-duration energy storage (LDES), or grid-scale storage technologies
Manufacturing background in energy hardware (solar panels, wind turbines, batteries, or electrolyzers)
Experience leading IPO readiness or major fundraising rounds ($200M+)

responsibilities: Lead Form Energy through commercialisation of iron-air battery technology and first utility-scale deployments
Drive manufacturing scale-up of Weirton, WV factory from pilot to GWh-scale production
Manage relationships with utility customers, DOE Loan Programs Office, and state regulators
Oversee company strategy, P&L, and team growth from 500 to 1,500+ employees
Represent company to board of directors, investors (Breakthrough Energy Ventures, ArcelorMittal), and media

expected_output.summary: Energy executive with 22 years at NextEra Energy, most recently SVP of Development & Engineering for NextEra Energy Resources (utility-scale renewables). Managed $4B annual capital deployment across wind, solar, and battery storage projects. Led development of 8 GW renewable portfolio across 15 US states. Deep relationships with utility executives, state PUCs, and FERC commissioners. Board member of American Clean Power Association. Seeking CEO role at growth-stage energy storage company.

experience.title: Senior Vice President — Development & Engineering
bullets: Led development and engineering organisation of 350 staff delivering 2-3 GW annually of wind, solar, and battery storage projects
Managed $4B annual capital deployment with P&L responsibility for project development pipeline
Secured IRA manufacturing credits (45X) and ITC allocations for 6 utility-scale battery storage projects (total 3.2 GWh)
Negotiated long-term PPAs and tolling agreements with 12 US utility customers (Duke Energy, Southern Company, Xcel Energy)
Testified before 4 state Public Utility Commissions on renewable energy procurement and grid reliability

experience.title: Vice President — Wind Development
bullets: Built NextEra's Midwest wind portfolio from 2 GW to 6 GW across 8 states
Managed relationships with GE Renewable Energy and Vestas for turbine supply agreements ($2B+ procurement)
Led due diligence and integration for 3 wind portfolio acquisitions (combined 1.5 GW)
Developed tax equity financing structures with JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, and Bank of America for PTC monetization

experience.title: Director, Business Development (promoted from Manager, 2008)
bullets: Originated and developed wind and solar projects across Texas, Oklahoma, and California
Built financial models for project economics, PPA pricing, and tax equity structures
Managed environmental permitting and land acquisition for 15+ wind farm sites

education: MBA — Wharton School of Business, University of Pennsylvania (2002-2004)
BS Mechanical Engineering — Georgia Institute of Technology (1998-2002)

jd_requirements: 20+ years energy utilities energy storage progressive leadership
manufacturing pilot to commercial production scaling
utility executives PUCs FERC DOE relationships
board governance public company PE VC
manufacturing energy hardware solar wind batteries electrolyzers
deliberate_flaws: Never held CEO, President, or COO title — highest is SVP (title gap for C-suite hire)
No manufacturing experience — NextEra develops and operates, doesn't manufacture hardware
No experience scaling factory operations (pilot to GWh production) — JD's core challenge
No iron-air or LDES technology experience — battery storage experience is lithium-ion procurement, not manufacturing
No public company board seat (industry association board only — American Clean Power)
No IPO or major fundraising experience — NextEra is publicly traded, different capital raising model
No experience managing manufacturing workforce (factory operations vs project development teams)
No PE/VC board governance experience — publicly traded utility company background
No startup or growth-stage company experience — entire career at one large company (NextEra/FPL)
No DOE Loan Programs Office relationship (FERC and PUCs yes, DOE LPO no)
